facts.claim: Full name: Sompal Kami. Born 2 February 1996.
Born in Chandrakot, Gulmi District, Lumbini Province, Nepal.
Born into a poor Dalit family in rural Gulmi.
Family migrated to Punjab, India shortly after his birth, seeking work. Father earned approximately INR 10,000 monthly.
Grew up in India playing cricket informally on streets in Punjab. Attended local schools in Punjab.
Returned to Nepal alone at age 17 in 2013 to pursue cricket, supported by the Nepali Cricket Fan Club.
Nicknames: Gulmi Express, Somu.
Bowling style: Right-arm fast-medium. Batting: Right-handed. Role: Bowling all-rounder.
Bowling speed up to 140 km/h, recorded at 2014 ICC World Twenty20.
T20I debut: 16 March 2014 vs Hong Kong. Figures: 2/20.
ODI debut: 1 August 2018 vs Netherlands. Figures: 2/48.
First Nepali bowler to take a five-wicket haul in an ODI: 26 January 2019 vs UAE, figures 5/33.
T20I career (as of early 2026): 88 matches, 84 wickets, best bowling 3/20, economy 7.17, bowling average 23.46.
ODI career (as of early 2026): 66 matches, 86 wickets, best bowling 5/33, economy 5.29, bowling average 29.90. Batting: 776 runs, highest score 67.
2014 Asian Games: Only Nepali player chosen in the Select XI of the tournament by the Asian Cricket Council. 5 wickets at average 4.00, economy 2.50.
First hat-trick in T20s: 26 March 2024 vs Ireland Wolves.
Represented Nepal at 2014 and 2024 T20 World Cups; selected for 2026 edition.
Received central contract from Nepal Cricket Association: September 2020.
2019 South Asian Games: Bronze medal winner with Nepal cricket team.
Shoulder injury in 2019 sidelined him for approximately six months.

Has played franchise cricket for: Karnali Yaks (NPL, captain), Winnipeg Hawks (Global T20 Canada, 2019 title winner — first Nepali to win an overseas franchise league), Maratha Arabians (Abu Dhabi T10), Flying Oryx (Qatar T10), Chitwan Tigers, Butwal Blasters.

flagged_gaps: Exact shoulder injury date/match in 2019 not confirmed by Tier 1 source — marked unverified
